hyperedges.label: Prior art systems referenced in design rationale
Core stack technologies chosen for LLM-buildability

nodes.label: No email anywhere — username+password auth, admin/CLI reset
nodes.norm_label: no email anywhere — username+password auth, admin/cli reset

nodes.label: No API key is ever required
nodes.norm_label: no api key is ever required

nodes.label: Browser loads fonts/artwork only from our own origin
nodes.norm_label: browser loads fonts/artwork only from our own origin

nodes.label: Every component consumes theme tokens only
nodes.norm_label: every component consumes theme tokens only

nodes.label: Anime is ContentProfile.ANIME, not a MediaKind
nodes.norm_label: anime is contentprofile.anime, not a mediakind

nodes.label: Confidence is derived from Evidence, never authored
nodes.norm_label: confidence is derived from evidence, never authored

nodes.label: Valkey is a cache, not a source of truth
nodes.norm_label: valkey is a cache, not a source of truth

nodes.label: Every job is idempotent, keyed on content hash
nodes.norm_label: every job is idempotent, keyed on content hash

nodes.label: Bind mounts only; no named docker volumes (except Postgres UID)
nodes.norm_label: bind mounts only; no named docker volumes (except postgres uid)

nodes.label: Every playback start creates a PlaybackSession
nodes.norm_label: every playback start creates a playbacksession

nodes.label: contentProfile is a default, not a hard wall, for MediaKind.MOVIE
nodes.norm_label: contentprofile is a default, not a hard wall, for mediakind.movie

nodes.label: Never block, but stay fixable principle
nodes.norm_label: never block, but stay fixable principle

nodes.label: Decided stack: TS/Node22, Fastify, Prisma, BullMQ+Valkey, React, Vidstack, JASSUB, custom ffmpeg
nodes.norm_label: decided stack: ts/node22, fastify, prisma, bullmq+valkey, react, vidstack, jassub, custom ffmpeg

nodes.label: Rejected: Go backend, NestJS, s6-overlay, Postgres-as-queue, anitomy-js
nodes.norm_label: rejected: go backend, nestjs, s6-overlay, postgres-as-queue, anitomy-js

nodes.label: Work the build order in docs/design.md §19
nodes.norm_label: work the build order in docs/design.md §19

nodes.label: Working agreement: small commits, ask when ambiguous, update doc explicitly
nodes.norm_label: working agreement: small commits, ask when ambiguous, update doc explicitly

nodes.label: Font store primitive
nodes.rationale: The browser only ever loads fonts from our own origin; where the server got the bytes is unconstrained. Same primitive as artwork's 'fetch once' rule — buys COOP/COEP immunity (§13.3) and privacy without hotlinking a third party on every page load.

nodes.label: No music, ever
nodes.rationale: Removes ID3 parsing, gapless playback, album/artist/track hierarchy, MusicBrainz's 1 req/sec limit, and the entire non-video UI fork.

nodes.label: No email, anywhere
nodes.rationale: Email/SMTP is the #1 source of self-hosted setup pain. Password reset is an admin action or CLI; invites are codes shared manually.

nodes.label: No API key ever required
nodes.rationale: A single shared project key creates a single point of failure — Jellyfin's bundled TMDB key meant one revocation broke every install. Local-first + keyless is the default; an optional user-supplied-key tier ships disabled, settings-only, never nagged.

nodes.label: License firewall
nodes.rationale: No AGPL-encumbered or non-commercial-restricted data or code in the core repo, ever, not even temporarily. packages/metadata ships interfaces only; encumbered adapters live in packages-optional/, fetched at runtime by the operator — a materially different license posture than redistribution.

nodes.label: Collections — movies inside series
nodes.rationale: The naive 'movie is top-level, series contains episodes' model is wrong for franchises like Demon Slayer: Mugen Train. Membership is many-to-many; both release_order and story_order are stored; anchor positions a movie inline in a series.

nodes.label: Evidence — the confidence engine
nodes.rationale: Confidence is derived, never stored as a magic number, so re-resolution is cheap and auditable and enables self-healing when new evidence arrives.

nodes.label: Generated artwork pipeline
nodes.rationale: Every item always has artwork; generation is the terminal, never-failing step. Backdrops extract well from frames; posters must be composed (blur-extend or weighted crop) because no 2:3 image exists inside a 16:9 film; logos are simply absent.

nodes.label: Job robustness (crash-only queue design)
nodes.rationale: Valkey is a cache, not a source of truth — losing it must lose zero work because a boot reconciler re-enqueues anything missing from Postgres-derived state. Every job is idempotent, keyed on content hash. Stricter than Immich, the right call for a homelab where Valkey isn't on stable storage.

nodes.label: Theming (token contract mechanism)
nodes.rationale: Token contract written before any UI. Every shadcn component consumes tokens only, never a hardcoded value — the rule that makes or breaks 100% themeability.

nodes.label: Bind mounts only, no named volumes
nodes.rationale: Named volumes hide data in /var/lib/docker/volumes, making backup opaque and migration painful. Postgres is the one documented exception: its own internal UID owns /config/db rather than fighting it with PUID/PGID.
nodes.norm_label: bind mounts only, no named volumes

nodes.label: Chromecast permanently out
nodes.rationale: Chromecast is hardcoded to Google's DNS and requires a publicly-resolvable, publicly-certificated host, which conflicts with Tailscale-only deployment and forces subtitle burn-in. No public domain is ever in scope, so this settles both Chromecast (closed) and AirPlay (deferred to native clients).

nodes.label: Playback decision engine (Direct Play/Stream/Transcode)
nodes.rationale: Client sends a device profile; server decides Direct Play vs Direct Stream vs Transcode, following Jellyfin's StreamBuilder evaluation order. The device-profile abstraction is kept general so a future 'airplay' profile is just subtitles: burn.

nodes.label: Eager font extraction at scan time
nodes.rationale: ASS styles reference fonts attached inside the MKV; not extracting means subs fall back to a default font. hokago extracts eagerly at scan time, deduped by hash into the shared font store, avoiding Jellyfin's repeated lazy-extraction bugs.
nodes.norm_label: eager font extraction at scan time

nodes.label: Media segments cascade (intro/outro skip)
nodes.rationale: Cascade cheapest-first: named chapters (free, instant) → community DB (keyless enrichment) → chromaprint (expensive, off-peak) → manual (always wins). Inverts Jellyfin's model, which runs expensive chromaprint analysis first.

nodes.label: Anime as ContentProfile, not MediaKind
nodes.rationale: Anime is a series/episode/movie with a different provider order and parser, not its own media_kind. contentProfile is the default provider order, not a hard boundary — for MOVIE specifically the resolver may also try the anime chain regardless of library profile, reusing the existing Evidence model rather than adding per-item profile inference.

nodes.label: Stack choice for LLM-buildability
nodes.rationale: One language end to end (TypeScript/Node 22), conventional over clever, explicit over magic, schema as source of truth — criteria chosen so an LLM can build and complete the codebase reliably.

nodes.label: packages-optional runtime-fetched adapter boundary
nodes.rationale: Deliberately empty in the core repo. AGPL-encumbered and non-commercial-restricted datasets are never vendored, bundled, or committed — an operator who wants them fetches the adapter at runtime onto their own instance, a materially different license posture than hokago redistributing them. Adapters here implement packages/metadata interfaces and nothing else in the core repo may depend on this directory.
